jokes.text: Do I look like Roc? I don't know, Carlton, maybe if you shaved your head bald... and sanded down the square part.
jokes.setup: Carlton objects to taking out garbage by referencing Roc (a sitcom character)
jokes.punchline: Will's elaborate description of what Carlton would need to do to look like Roc
jokes.explanation: Sharp character-specific roast that plays on Carlton's appearance and preppy nature versus the working-class Roc character

jokes.text: Because I'm bigger than you. See, that's not fair. That's only 'cause you got them shoe lifts in today.
jokes.setup: Will uses his size advantage to make Ashley take out trash
jokes.punchline: Ashley calling out Will for wearing shoe lifts
jokes.explanation: Solid sibling banter with Ashley getting one over on Will

jokes.text: They're not lifts. I doubled up on my Odor-Eaters.
jokes.setup: Will denying he wears shoe lifts
jokes.punchline: His equally embarrassing excuse about foot odor products
jokes.explanation: Good economy and character consistency - Will trying to save face but making it worse

jokes.text: You know, those things you hit every time you back out the car.
jokes.setup: Hilary asking where to take the trash
jokes.punchline: Will pointing out her terrible driving
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - plays on Hilary's airheadedness and bad driving

jokes.text: Thank God. I thought it was the neighbor's kids.
jokes.setup: Learning she hits the trash cans when backing up
jokes.punchline: Hilary's relief that she's not hitting children
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Hilary's obliviousness taken to dark extreme with perfect delivery

jokes.text: Well, just pretend it's buying you dinner afterwards.
jokes.setup: Hilary saying she doesn't touch greasy, disgusting things
jokes.punchline: Will's sexual innuendo about her dating life
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that turns Hilary's complaint into a dig at her dating standards

jokes.text: And for your information, dinner comes first.
jokes.setup: Will's sexual innuendo
jokes.punchline: Hilary correcting the order while missing the point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - she's oblivious to the innuendo but has to correct the etiquette

jokes.text: Good morning, Will. Good morning, Uncle Phil. Here's your lunch.
jokes.setup: Will hands Uncle Phil something
jokes.punchline: The trash bag is Uncle Phil's 'lunch'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual gag with perfect timing - plays on Uncle Phil's size while resolving the trash situation

jokes.text: Actually, sir, I have an impacted wisdom tooth... my hair is falling out, and I have a bunion the size of my fist. Oh, no. We're out of Mrs. Butterworth's. Thanks for the talk, big guy.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil asking Geoffrey about the glorious day
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's litany of problems dismissed when Uncle Phil finds a bigger crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect character work showing Uncle Phil's self-absorption and Geoffrey's resignation

jokes.text: Hey, look, any chance of you letting me balance this book on your head?
jokes.setup: Will is rushing around frantically
jokes.punchline: Will asks to use Uncle Phil's large head as a book stand
jokes.explanation: Classic Will fat joke about Uncle Phil, delivered with characteristic boldness

jokes.text: Hey, you know I was gonna put the waffles up there... but that's just a little too close to your mouth, you know what I mean?
jokes.setup: Will asking to balance a book on Uncle Phil's head
jokes.punchline: Will's crack about Uncle Phil's eating habits
jokes.explanation: Classic Will fat joke about Uncle Phil with good setup and delivery

jokes.text: I mean, between my school work and Cindy... and, you know, my job and Cindy... and, you know, basketball and Cindy... Wow, you think I'm neglecting Cindy?
jokes.setup: Will listing all his busy activities
jokes.punchline: Cindy appears in every item, leading to the obvious conclusion
jokes.explanation: Good escalating structure with Will's self-aware punchline

jokes.text: It's nice to know that all my labors haven't gone unnoticed. I was talking to Will. And I, apparently, was talking to myself.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey thinking Uncle Phil appreciated his work
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dejected realization he was ignored
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey moment of being overlooked with perfect timing

jokes.text: Well, judging by the size of that thing, you must be going out with Nell Carter.
jokes.setup: Carlton's big pimple before his big date
jokes.punchline: Will's comparison to large comedian Nell Carter
jokes.explanation: Sharp pop culture reference with perfect economy connecting pimple size to date quality

jokes.text: Girl, anybody ever told you that you got the... Thanks, I'll try not to let it go to my head.
jokes.setup: Will starting to compliment Cindy
jokes.punchline: Cindy cutting him off and responding to the implied compliment
jokes.explanation: Nice timing gag with Cindy's confidence interrupting Will's smooth talk

jokes.text: I'm planning on staying up all night, you know... 'cause "all I want to do is zoom-a-zoom-zoom-zoom."
jokes.setup: Will promising to have energy for prom
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo via song lyrics
jokes.explanation: Typical Will sexual innuendo through pop culture reference

jokes.text: "Smith dribbles. He stops. He pops. He scores. The crowd goes wild!"
jokes.setup: Will talking about the big basketball game
jokes.punchline: Will acting out his fantasy sports commentary
jokes.explanation: Classic Will showboating with physical comedy

jokes.text: No, that's all right. I don't think... listening to Bruce Springsteen got the same effect on black people.
jokes.setup: Someone suggesting what they do to stay energized
jokes.punchline: Will's racial observation about Bruce Springsteen
jokes.explanation: Smart cultural observation that's character-specific and well-constructed

jokes.text: My breath ain't stinking, so I'm guessing them ain't Tic Tacs.
jokes.setup: Someone offering Will pills
jokes.punchline: Will's street-smart deduction about what they really are
jokes.explanation: Good street logic from Will with economical delivery

jokes.text: To me, it's just freeze-dried coffee, just in case. Just in case.
jokes.setup: Jazz explaining his casual drug use
jokes.punchline: The repeated 'just in case' showing his rationalization
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate rationalization with dark undertones

jokes.text: I just want to thank Bill and Hillary Clinton... for inviting me to the inauguration.
jokes.setup: Will appearing to give an acceptance speech
jokes.punchline: Random reference to political event while exhausted
jokes.explanation: Absurd non-sequitur showing Will's exhausted state

jokes.text: I just want to thank Bill and Hillary Clinton... for inviting me to the inauguration.
jokes.setup: Will falling asleep standing up
jokes.punchline: Will dreams he's at a presidential inauguration
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's exhaustion through absurd dream content, topical for 1993

jokes.text: also known as "guaranteed action night."
jokes.setup: Carlton mentioning senior prom
jokes.punchline: Carlton's overly confident alternate name for prom night
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - shows his awkward confidence about romance

jokes.text: G, it's coffee. No, it's Folgers Crystals.
jokes.setup: Will pointing out Geoffrey's remedy is just coffee
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's brand-specific correction
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Geoffrey's pretentious specificity

jokes.text: You see the volcano-sized pimple about to erupt on my forehead... and you don't think vitamin E is strong enough... This sex thing is even more complicated than I thought.
jokes.setup: Carlton thinking Will is asking about sexual enhancement drugs
jokes.punchline: Carlton's innocent misunderstanding about drug purposes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton innocence meeting serious topic with great character work

jokes.text: This sex thing is even more complicated than I thought.
jokes.setup: Will asking about drugs to help you stay 'up'
jokes.punchline: Carlton thinks Will is asking about erectile dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton innocence moment - multiple levels of misunderstanding create great comedy

jokes.text: Yo, man, I'm just sharing a limo with you.
jokes.setup: Carlton talking about sharing the big night with someone who cares
jokes.punchline: Will clarifies he's only sharing transportation, not romance
jokes.explanation: Good reality check moment from Will, deflates Carlton's romantic moment

jokes.text: No, man, she's just looking for loose change.
jokes.setup: Carlton saying his date worships the ground he walks on
jokes.punchline: Will's literal interpretation
jokes.explanation: Good literal interpretation joke with economical delivery

jokes.text: Somebody call a cop, 'cause it got to be illegal to look that good.
jokes.setup: Cindy looking great for prom
jokes.punchline: Will's over-the-top compliment
jokes.explanation: Standard smooth-talking Will line, somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Girl, don't hate me 'cause I'm beautiful.
jokes.setup: Cindy complimenting Will's appearance
jokes.punchline: Will's reference to Pantene commercial
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference typical of Will's character

jokes.text: No, shoot me from the other side. I've got a situation on my forehead.
jokes.setup: Photo opportunity before prom
jokes.punchline: Carlton's euphemistic reference to his pimple
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Carlton's proper language for embarrassing topic

jokes.text: Come on, we'll be home early. Early tomorrow morning.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil asking about curfew
jokes.punchline: Will's clarification about when 'early' means
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection joke about prom night expectations

jokes.text: Hey, yo, I'm telling you, this is gonna be the most exciting night of our lives.
jokes.setup: Will's enthusiastic prediction about prom night
jokes.punchline: Dramatic irony since audience knows Carlton will overdose
jokes.explanation: Works better on rewatch when you know the irony. Standard prom excitement line becomes ominous.

jokes.text: Stare at hers. Yeah, to go along with the biggest zit of your life.
jokes.setup: Carlton worried about his date staring at his pimples
jokes.punchline: Will's practical but cruel advice and follow-up
jokes.explanation: Mean but character-appropriate advice from Will

jokes.text: you can just pull your big ol' bottom lip up over your head.
jokes.setup: Will telling Carlton to check his locker for pills
jokes.punchline: Will's absurd threat/insult
jokes.explanation: Absurd Will threat that's more silly than threatening

jokes.text: How about "I'm going home"? Now that's the jam. Tell me when he puts that on.
jokes.setup: Will saying he's waiting for the DJ to play something he likes
jokes.punchline: Cindy suggesting leaving, Will enthusiastically agreeing
jokes.explanation: Good relationship humor showing Will's exhaustion affecting his date

jokes.text: Even though I was feeling insecure before, I realized it doesn't matter, anyway... 'cause beauty's only skin deep. It's what's inside that counts. Vanessa, you drive me wild. I hope this night never ends. Is it just me or are my lips moving really, really fast?
jokes.setup: Carlton having taken speed unknowingly
jokes.punchline: His rapid-fire philosophical then romantic then self-aware commentary
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work showing Carlton on speed - philosophical, romantic, then self-aware

jokes.text: Is it just me or are my lips moving really, really fast?
jokes.setup: Carlton's drug-induced confidence speech
jokes.punchline: Carlton notices his speed-affected speech patterns
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy detail showing drug effects, Carlton's innocent observation

jokes.text: I guess he's got that kind of natural speed thing going. Yeah, you can say that again.
jokes.setup: Cindy commenting on Carlton's energy
jokes.punchline: Will's knowing agreement with unintended double meaning
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic irony - Cindy doesn't know Carlton literally took speed

jokes.text: I'll go to Church every Sunday. I'll even give up women. Will, was that you? You know I was just kidding about that woman part, right?
jokes.setup: Will making desperate promises to God
jokes.punchline: Immediately backtracking on the women promise when Carlton wakes up
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will character moment - even in crisis, priorities are clear

jokes.text: Last thing I remember, I was doing the running man... to I Will Always Love You.
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to remember what happened
jokes.punchline: The absurd image of Carlton doing hip-hop dance to Whitney Houston
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific absurdity - Carlton's innocent dance style with romantic ballad

jokes.text: I never thought losing my virginity would be this painful.
jokes.setup: Carlton feeling terrible in the hospital
jokes.punchline: Carlton thinking his condition is from losing his virginity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton innocence - connecting hospital stay to sexual experience

jokes.text: What could be worse than finding out I'm still a virgin? It was speed. Oh, my God. I'm a drug addict and a virgin!
jokes.setup: Carlton asking what could be worse than being a virgin
jokes.punchline: Learning about the drugs makes both conditions equally tragic to him
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation and character work - Carlton's priorities perfectly revealed

jokes.text: Oh, my God. I'm a drug addict and a virgin!
jokes.setup: Will revealing Carlton took speed
jokes.punchline: Carlton combines both pieces of bad news into one horrified realization
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton reaction - combines both his concerns into one panicked outburst, highly quotable

jokes.text: We still have to mix his aspirin in with applesauce.
jokes.setup: Will defending Carlton as not a drug user
jokes.punchline: Revealing Carlton's childlike medication habits
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail with excellent economy - shows Carlton's innocence perfectly

jokes.text: Just say you didn't inhale.
jokes.setup: Carlton worrying about political future after drug incident
jokes.punchline: Reference to Bill Clinton's marijuana admission
jokes.explanation: Topical political reference with good timing

jokes.text: Just say you didn't inhale.
jokes.setup: Carlton worried about political aspirations after drug incident
jokes.punchline: Nurse references Bill Clinton's famous marijuana excuse
jokes.explanation: Topical political humor referencing Clinton's famous quote, well-timed for 1993

jokes.text: Do I? / Can we stick to the subject, please?
jokes.setup: Nurse asking if Will looks like a drug addict
jokes.punchline: Will's appearance apparently suggests he might, Phil quickly redirects
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag implying Will's street appearance, Phil's quick redirect is protective

jokes.text: Hell, I've been from Yale to jail, and from Park Avenue to park bench... but now I'm clean and sober. That's really touching... but do you think you can get me a nurse without a record?
jokes.setup: Patient sharing his recovery story
jokes.punchline: Will's insensitive request for a different nurse
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's privileged perspective clashing with reality

jokes.text: As a matter of fact, I think this is the one and only time.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil saying he doesn't often get to compliment Will
jokes.punchline: Clarifying this might be the only time ever
jokes.explanation: Good Uncle Phil character moment with backhanded compliment structure

jokes.text: Honey, I don't think we can both fit in that bed. Right, and a chair for my wife.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil and Aunt Viv planning to stay overnight
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil's size making bed-sharing impossible
jokes.explanation: Standard Uncle Phil size joke with good delivery

jokes.text: I didn't get much sleep last night with all that kicking.
jokes.setup: Family discussing spending the night at hospital
jokes.punchline: Phil reveals Vivian kicks in her sleep
jokes.explanation: Light marital humor showing domestic reality, not particularly clever

jokes.text: Thanks a lot for covering for me, man. But why did you do it? I don't know. I think I was still high.
jokes.setup: Carlton asking why Will covered for him with the family
jokes.punchline: Will joking he was still under influence of drugs
jokes.explanation: Will deflecting with humor to avoid the serious emotional moment. Character-appropriate avoidance.

jokes.text: Thanks a lot for covering for me, man. But why did you do it? I don't know. I think I was still high.
jokes.setup: Carlton asking why Will covered for him
jokes.punchline: Will's throwaway explanation about still being high
jokes.explanation: Casual Will deflection with drug reference callback

jokes.text: Hey, I've got a wild side. I don't always wear shower shoes at the gym.
jokes.setup: Carlton claiming to have a wild side
jokes.punchline: His idea of wildness is not wearing shower shoes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton character moment - his 'wild side' is hilariously tame and specific

jokes.text: You haven't showered yet today, have you?
jokes.setup: Will hugging Carlton emotionally
jokes.punchline: Carlton's practical concern about Will's hygiene
jokes.explanation: Good Carlton character moment deflating emotional scene with practical concern

jokes.text: I can't take this, Uncle Phil.
jokes.setup: Phil giving Will expensive Clippers season tickets
jokes.punchline: Will's reaction suggests the weight of his guilt
jokes.explanation: More dramatic beat than comedic, shows Will's internal conflict

jokes.text: Somebody call a cop 'cause it has got to be... Somebody call a cop 'cause this has... you'll get it.
jokes.setup: Will trying to repeat his earlier compliment line
jokes.punchline: Giving up and breaking the fourth wall
jokes.explanation: Meta callback to earlier joke with fourth-wall break typical of the show

jokes.text: Welcome to the Will Fresh Prince Ever Say His Line contest.
jokes.setup: Will's failed attempts at his signature line
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall with fake game show reference
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor and wordplay on show's title, typical of Fresh Prince style
